danger_signals.visual_form: Dark colors, skeletal imagery, gothic architecture in 'Stones 'N Bones' table
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard/challenge zone; players must complete dangerous mode progressions

danger_signals.visual_form: Physical bumper targets with death/skeleton theming
danger_signals.meaning: Obstacles requiring precision hits; failure results in ball loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Multi-path ramp layouts with varying visual prominence
danger_signals.meaning: Skill-based challenge zones; misalignment results in unfavorable outcomes

safety_signals.visual_form: Special ramp/target completion sequences lighting up
safety_signals.meaning: Achievement milestone; grants additional chances to continue play

safety_signals.visual_form: Mode-specific visual feedback when objectives are met
safety_signals.meaning: Safe/stable game state; player has earned access to bonus point opportunities

safety_signals.visual_form: Vibrant palette in 'Party Land' table with carnival theming

faction_indicators.color_identity: Bright, high-saturation colors typical of carnival/fair settings
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Amusement park structures, festive decorations, accessible ramp layouts

faction_indicators.color_identity: Dynamic racing palette with speed-suggesting color transitions
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Car overtaking mechanics; Miles counter visual indicator
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opponent car AI positioning relative to player
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Racetrack-style table layout; racing-themed bumpers and ramps

faction_indicators.color_identity: Game show aesthetics with prize-indicating colors
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Ramp combination sequences; prize claim mechanics
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Gameshow set styling; ramp bank organization for prize-tier unlocking

faction_indicators.color_identity: Dark, desaturated palette; purples, blacks, grays
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Gothic architecture; haunted house decoration; eight-mode progression structure

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Party Land table presented as entry point; bright, forgiving aesthetics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Simple objective communication (PARTY/CRAZY letters); accessible ramp geometry

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Speed Devils and Gameshow tables; increased visual complexity in ramp layouts
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Multi-objective tracking (Miles, Skills counters); higher precision requirements

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stones 'N Bones table; dark, complex visual field; eight-mode progression display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cyclical mode completion requirement; sustained difficulty maintenance; high-scoring round gating

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Game state reset indicated through loss of accumulated mode progress
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Bonus scores excluded; cyclical mode advancement reversed; mechanical penalty applied

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Extra ball award trigger; visual confirmation of threshold breach
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Immediate ball recovery; extended play opportunity granted

environmental_storytelling.example: Stones 'N Bones uses gothic structure, skeletal imagery, and dark color palette to communicate haunted house narrative without dialogue; Party Land uses carnival colors and PARTY/CRAZY letter sequences to establish cheerful amusement park context

Speed Devils table displays 'Miles' counter and car-overtaking visuals; Gameshow table presents prize-claim ramp sequences; narrative identity emerges through mechanical representation rather than cutscene

Stones 'N Bones reveals eight progressively rewarding modes through target bank completion; visual complexity and ramp difficulty increase with mode advancement, communicating escalating challenge through gameplay architecture

Each table includes running-total visual indicators (Cyclones, Miles, Skills, Screams) that communicate accumulated progress; first-shot bonus (2-point multiplier) indicated through visual prominence or highlighting

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Follows pinball mechanics; responds to ramp geometry and bumper placement; trajectory communicates table design intent
Positional changes relative to player ball position; overtaking mechanics determine mode progression
Visually inactive; require precision hits to activate
Visual feedback indicates successful completion; mode progression unlocked; ramp routing changes
Visually gated; require specific objective thresholds (Miles, Skills numbers) to activate

notes: Pinball Fantasies SNES implements world-state communication primarily through thematic table design, visual palette differentiation, and mechanical objective systems. Danger/safety signals are implicit in table aesthetic (haunted vs. carnival) and gameplay difficulty rather than explicit danger HUD elements. Faction identity is expressed through four distinct thematic environments with unique color schemes, objectives, and narrative contexts. Political situations are absent; game focuses on competitive scoring rather than faction dynamics. Limited SNES color palette constrains visual differentiation compared to Amiga version.
